verdict.answer_first: B leads with the fix: "The main problem is the unguarded property access"; A starts softer: "Yes, a few things".
verdict.decisiveness: B gives one primary correction with a safer version; A splits into "1", "2", "Minor" and ends by asking another question.
verdict.fabrication: A overstates: "propagates as an unhandled rejection unless the caller wraps it"; propagation is not automatically unhandled. B: none.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to apply: it names the failure, gives the patch, then only the key design caveat.
